deep_dive.title: ASPIRE: Agentic /Skills Discovery for Robotics
deep_dive.abstract: Traditional robot programming is challenging: it requires orchestrating multimodal perception, managing physical contact dynamics, and handling diverse configurations and execution failures. We introduce ASPIRE (Agentic Skill Programming through Iterative Robot Exploration), a continual learning system that autonomously writes and refines robot control programs in a code-as-policy paradigm while compounding experience into a reusable skill library. ASPIRE discovers skills that persist across tasks, simulation and real-world settings, and embodiments. It operates in an open-ended loop with three components: (1) a closed-loop robot execution engine that exposes fine-grained multimodal traces, enabling autonomous failure diagnosis, repair synthesis, and validation; (2) a continually expanding skill library that distills validated fixes into reusable, transferable knowledge; and (3) evolutionary search that generates diverse task sequences and control programs to explore beyond single-trajectory refinement. ASPIRE surpasses prior methods by up to 77% on LIBERO-Pro manipulation under perturbation, 72% on Robosuite bimanual handover, and 32% on BEHAVIOR-1K long-horizon household tasks. Its accumulated library also enables zero-shot generalization to unseen long-horizon tasks: on LIBERO-Pro Long, ASPIRE achieves 31% success versus 4% for prior methods despite their use of test-time reasoning and retries. Finally, simulation-discovered skills provide initial evidence of sim-to-real transfer, substantially reducing real-robot programming effort across different embodiments and robot APIs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Continual learning system for robot programming with multimodal perception and execution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具多模态感知和执行的机器人持续学习系统
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具多模态感知和执行的机器人持续学习系统
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具多模态感知和执行的机器人持续学习系统
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ASPIRE autonomously writes and refines robot control programs, building a reusable skill library through iterative exploration
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出ASPIRE系统，使机器人能够自主编写和精炼控制程序（代码即策略范式），通过持续探索积累经验，形成可跨任务、跨本体复用的技能库。系统包含闭环执行引擎、技能库和迭代机制，在仿真和真实机器人上展示了任务泛化能力。其核心是将AI与物理传感器执行器深度集成，属于具身智能范畴。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出ASPIRE系统，使机器人能够自主编写和精炼控制程序（代码即策略范式），通过持续探索积累经验，形成可跨任务、跨本体复用的技能库。系统包含闭环执行引擎、技能库和迭代机制，在仿真和真实机器人上展示了任务泛化能力。其核心是将AI与物理传感器执行器深度集成，属于具身智能范畴。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: artificial intelligence integrated with physical agents and sensors

quick_skim.title: Learning from Demonstration via Spatiotemporal Tubes for Unknown Euler-Lagrange Systems
quick_skim.abstract: We present STT-LfD, a unified Learning from Demonstration (LfD) framework that integrates motion learning with control for unknown Euler-Lagrange systems. Unlike traditional decoupled approaches that track a fixed reference, the proposed method treats demonstrations as a data-driven safety specification. Using heteroscedastic Gaussian Processes, STT-LfD learns Spatiotemporal Tubes (STTs) as an intent envelope that capture time-varying precision requirements of a task. A closed-form feedback controller then enforces these learned constraints while respecting actuator limits, without requiring explicit system identification. The approach preserves the temporal structure of demonstrations, remains computationally efficient, and avoids explicit system identification. Hardware experiments on a mobile robot and a 7-DOF manipulator show that it outperforms baselines in robustness to disturbances and computational speed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Learning from demonstration framework for unknown systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从演示中学习的统一框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从演示中学习的统一框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从演示中学习的统一框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: STT-LfD integrates motion learning with control by treating demonstrations as safety specifications via spatiotemporal tubes, enabling efficient imitation without system identification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对未知欧拉-拉格朗日系统，现有从演示学习（LfD）方法通常跟踪固定参考轨迹，忽略了演示的时间变化精度需求。本文提出STT-LfD框架，利用异方差高斯过程学习时空管（STT）作为意图包络，并通过闭环反馈控制器强制执行学习到的约束，无需系统辨识。实验表明该方法能有效保留演示的时间结构且计算高效，为机器人操控中的模仿学习提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对未知欧拉-拉格朗日系统，现有从演示学习（LfD）方法通常跟踪固定参考轨迹，忽略了演示的时间变化精度需求。本文提出STT-LfD框架，利用异方差高斯过程学习时空管（STT）作为意图包络，并通过闭环反馈控制器强制执行学习到的约束，无需系统辨识。实验表明该方法能有效保留演示的时间结构且计算高效，为机器人操控中的模仿学习提供了新思路。
